steps.title: Find the slope from the graph
steps.content:
From the graph, line $k$ passes through $(0,2)$ and $(4,-3)$. Its slope is
$$m_k=\frac{-3-2}{4-0}=-\frac{5}{4}$$

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: A perpendicular line has the negative reciprocal slope, so line $j$ has slope $\frac{4}{5}$.

steps.title: Solve for the intercept
steps.content:
Line $j$ passes through $(19,23)$. Substitute into $y=mx+b$.
$$23=\frac{4}{5}(19)+b$$
$$b=23-\frac{76}{5}=\frac{39}{5}$$
The answer is C.
